rationale: In the editorial's special-issue discussion, Kim et al. (2022) is not only listed but also summarized for its specific evidence: AI-assisted tutoring improves student outcomes, with gains limited by tutor reluctance to use the tools. The editors use this study to support a broader argument about technology-enabled service delivery and supplier-side adoption frictions in education markets. This gives the citation a clear conceptual role in framing the issue's contributions, beyond a purely passing reference.
relation_description: The citing editorial uses Kim et al. (2022) as a concrete empirical exemplar to argue that educational technology can improve outcomes but faces frontline adoption barriers. It synthesizes that paper's findings to position the special issue's narrative on technological change in education marketing.
